chapters.title: Heart Energy in the Body
chapters.description: From groundedness, the heart energy is invited to fill the body, with emphasis on tenderness, flexibility, and wishing ourselves well.

chapters.title: Care and the Wider Field
chapters.description: The reflection broadens into ways attention can express care for one’s life, with the meditation holding space for what arises.

chapters.title: Love over Hatred
chapters.description: A poetic sequence reframes hatred as something brittle and unnecessary, ending with the invitation to breathe and feel love directly.

title: Guided Meditation: What can you stop doing?
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver explores loving-kindness as something different from ordinary wanting: not a stronger preference, but a letting go of clinging, fear, and motivational desperation. He moves from Buddhist teaching to the felt realities of attachment in family and romantic love, then toward a more honest, less possessive way of caring that can include grief and dukkha without becoming overwhelmed by them. The talk closes by linking metta to moral elevation, rest, and a heart made less available to hatred.
short_summary: A guided reflection on metta as release from clinging, with honest attention to attachment, dukkha, and the quiet force of goodness.

chapters.title: Opening into metta
chapters.description: He begins by naming the week’s theme and setting up the central contrast between ordinary liking and love.

chapters.title: Love beyond wanting
chapters.description: Matthew argues that love is not just another form of wanting, but something that stands outside hedonic preference.

chapters.title: Wanting and fear
chapters.description: He uses the Buddha’s teaching on clinging to show how wanting and fear share a common motivational pressure that contracts the mind.

chapters.title: Metta as release
chapters.description: Loving-kindness is presented as a vast, receptive stance that appears when desperation and grasping fade.

chapters.title: Approaching what hurts
chapters.description: He describes love and mindfulness as forms of moving toward avoided experience so pain can unwind and lose its charge.

chapters.title: Kindness, not niceness
chapters.description: Matthew clarifies the kind of love he means by distinguishing kindness from mere social niceness and by naming the difference between generosity and possessiveness.

chapters.title: Attachment with grace
chapters.description: Using examples from parenting and romantic longing, he acknowledges that some attachment is irreducible and asks whether we can consent to its dukkha without utopian expectations.

chapters.title: Love without control
chapters.description: He turns toward a love that seeks no control, with metta arising through recognition of goodness, honesty, and moral beauty.

chapters.title: Abiding in goodness
chapters.description: The talk closes by linking loving-kindness to inner uplift, rest, and a heart that becomes less and less willing to live inside hatred and separation.

title: Guided Meditation: Putting Down the Project of Your Life
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation invites a gentle settling into posture, breath, and a quieter relationship to vigilance. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the sense that worry, defensiveness, and the “project called my life” can be set down for a moment, making room for tranquility and not knowing. The practice gradually opens into the felt relief of letting go and the capacities that arise with a calmer nervous system: love, kindness, compassion, forgiveness, care, and peace.
short_summary: A guided meditation on settling, softening vigilance, and briefly setting down the burden of life as a project.

chapters.title: Arriving and settling
chapters.description: After the opening welcome, the meditation begins with settling the body and allowing time before trying to assume any inner posture.

chapters.title: Relaxed, alert posture
chapters.description: He orients the body toward a balance of openness and alertness, encouraging patience with the settling process.

chapters.title: Patience with worry
chapters.description: The instruction turns toward the mind's wayward longings and the habit of worrying before trust has fully arrived.

chapters.title: Breath over vigilance
chapters.description: Breath is used as a way to signal safety to the body and loosen defensiveness, with worry contrasted against tranquility.

chapters.title: Tranquility and not knowing
chapters.description: He emphasizes surrendering to breathing, silence, and uncertainty, letting the mind rest outside hyper-arousal.

chapters.title: Putting down the project
chapters.description: A central teaching frames the project of one's life as a burden and invites a temporary release of that self-management.

chapters.title: Returning without carrying
chapters.description: The meditation stays with the simplicity of the moment, acknowledging that the project will be picked up again without needing to be held constantly.

chapters.title: Wholesome qualities emerge
chapters.description: In the quiet that follows, he links settling with the Dharma and names the relief of letting go alongside love, kindness, compassion, forgiveness, care, and peace.

title: Guided Meditation: It's all come to this
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on gathering the goodness of practice and meeting the present moment without violence. The talk moves from recollecting years of practice and the pain and wisdom that have led here, into a gentler way of relating to breath, body, and mind. He then turns toward refuge, showing how to rest in what is already here rather than trying to outthink uncertainty.
short_summary: A guided meditation on recollecting practice, softening into the present, and finding a deeper refuge in ordinary experience.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: He welcomes listeners and invites them to settle, alert but comfortable, for meditation.

chapters.title: Recollecting Practice
chapters.description: The meditation begins by recalling moments of willingness, retreat, practice, and friendship with the Dharma.

chapters.title: All Come To This
chapters.description: He gathers the whole arc of practice and life—pain, greed, hate, wisdom, and love—into the present moment.

chapters.title: Gently Tending Life
chapters.description: A long stretch of repeated phrases emphasizes tenderness, acceptance, and non-violence toward experience.

chapters.title: Ordinary Strategies
chapters.description: He names the ways we try to manage uncertainty through pleasure, planning, and rehashing the past.

chapters.title: Deeper Refuge
chapters.description: The meditation shifts toward refuge, resting in breath, body, and the present life as it is.

chapters.title: Winds Of Change
chapters.description: He closes by inviting trust in impermanence, letting change move through without harm.

title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Dharma Soak into You
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation invites a gentle, receptive relationship with practice rather than an effort to force it. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes settling into the body, meeting agitation with patience and quiet love, and tending tenderly to any sense of distance from the Dharma. The talk later turns to recollecting Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha as a way of re-entering sensitivity, urgency, and trust, before ending in a simple offering of the heart with no strings attached.
short_summary: A guided meditation on settling into the body, softening resistance, and letting recollection and care bring the Dharma closer.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation opens by finding some sweetness in sangha, technology, and lineage, then asks for a gradual settling of attention.

chapters.title: Body and Earth
chapters.description: Attention moves into the body on the earth, with repeated emphasis on gravity as a kind of embrace.

chapters.title: Meeting Unsettledness
chapters.description: Agitation, tension, and numbness are met with patience, tolerance, awareness, and quiet love.

chapters.title: When Dharma Feels Distant
chapters.description: Matthew names the felt remoteness of practice and invites listeners not to force connection, but to tend the sense of separation gently.

chapters.title: Recalling the Triple Gem
chapters.description: Recollecting Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha is presented as a way to remember, soften, and enter greater sensitivity and care.

chapters.title: Courage To Keep Looking
chapters.description: The meditation deepens into the courage of meeting this moment as it is, staying open to surprise rather than assuming we already know it.

chapters.title: Offering Without Strings
chapters.description: The close releases expectation and simply offers the heart to the Dharma, without insisting on any result.

title: Guided Meditation: Vitality and Release, In Breath, Out Breath
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how the in-breath and out-breath can be felt as different but complementary ways of practicing: vitality on the in-breath, release on the out-breath. He encourages a steady, simple attention that learns to welcome both alertness and surrender, and then extends that felt sense into the claim that this moment is not a dress rehearsal but the place where life is actually lived. The talk stays close to breath, posture, and the emotional textures of grasping, fear, brightness, and letting go.
short_summary: A guided meditation on balancing enlivening in-breaths with releasing out-breaths, and recognizing this moment as the place where life is lived.

chapters.title: Arriving and orienting
chapters.description: The meditation opens with a warm welcome and a check-in with the heart, then names the basic theme of learning the rhythms of practice.

chapters.title: Vitality and surrender
chapters.description: Brensilver introduces the idea that some practitioners lean toward the in-breath while others prefer the out-breath, framing maturity as learning to love both.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: He invites a comfortable posture and a bright, steady presence as the body breathes in and out.

chapters.title: The in-breath's vividness
chapters.description: Attention turns to the rush, brightness, and poignancy of inhalation, including the way it can disrupt monotony and remind us not to miss our lives.

chapters.title: Releasing the out-breath
chapters.description: The out-breath is explored as a letting go of tension, vigilance, fear, and subtle ownership, with repeated encouragement to breathe out.

chapters.title: Breath and clarity
chapters.description: The talk deepens into the breath cycle as a moment-by-moment expression of clarity, release, brightness, and darkness.

chapters.title: Letting go of becoming
chapters.description: Brensilver links the out-breath with softening the impulse to become someone slightly better, and the in-breath with shaking off the idea that this moment is only rehearsal.

chapters.title: This is our life
chapters.description: The closing section widens the frame: meditation is not separate from life, because life is found in breath, thought, feeling, seeing, and hearing here and now.

title: Guided Meditation: Vulnerability & Courage
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on vulnerability as a source of steadiness rather than weakness. The practice moves from settling the body and refreshing curiosity, into reflections on being undefended, allowing experience to pass through without hardening. He closes by linking courage to a balanced middle way and to a heart that can remain open without losing its center.
short_summary: A guided meditation on meeting experience with vulnerability, openness, and courage.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: He invites listeners to get cozy, but not too cozy, and to bring relaxed, energetic openness to body and mind.

chapters.title: Fresh Curiosity
chapters.description: The practice turns toward sincerity as a willingness to be surprised, with attention resting evenly in the present moment.

chapters.title: Breathing and Waking
chapters.description: A brief settling period emphasizes breathing, relaxing, and waking up to experience as it is.

chapters.title: Vulnerability as Strength
chapters.description: He reframes tenderness and vulnerability as the basis of real power, not brittle self-protection.

chapters.title: Undefended Heart
chapters.description: The talk deepens into the idea of being undefended without losing center, allowing softness, clarity, and love to coexist.

chapters.title: Attuning to Courage
chapters.description: Listeners are guided to sense their own power and to meet the moment with an open chest and a readiness to let feeling move through.

chapters.title: Middle Way of Courage
chapters.description: He brings in Aristotle’s image of courage between recklessness and cowardice, using it to point toward balanced, grounded openness.

chapters.title: Nothing to Defend
chapters.description: The meditation closes by suggesting that when there is no territory to protect, defensiveness softens and confidence remains.

title: Guided Meditation: Soothing, Space, Love
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with gentle self-care and bodily soothing, then gradually opens into spacious awareness. From there, he turns toward the sense of love as something subtle and already present, not something to manufacture. The practice closes by returning whatever arises — even fear — back into that larger field of love.
short_summary: A guided meditation moving from soothing the body into spacious awareness and an invitation to rest in love.

chapters.title: Arrival and Intention
chapters.description: The meditation opens with a brief welcome and an invitation to remember what matters in the heart.

chapters.title: Soothing the Body
chapters.description: Attention settles into simple, pleasant physical contact and a felt sense of care for the body.

chapters.title: Breath and Unfreezing
chapters.description: The breath is used to enliven the body and soften what feels braced or frozen.

chapters.title: Recognizing Vastness
chapters.description: The mind is invited to rest in openness and recognize spaciousness already present.

chapters.title: Space Without Struggle
chapters.description: He emphasizes that vastness does not need to be created and that solidity or density can be allowed without fighting it.

chapters.title: Love and Poignancy
chapters.description: The teaching turns toward a subtle sense of love and the tenderness of meeting life closely.

chapters.title: Returning to Love
chapters.description: Whatever arises, including fear, is received and offered back into love.

title: Guided Meditation: Thinking as coping
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how thinking can function as coping, especially when the system feels unsafe or unsettled. He gently leads listeners toward relaxing the body, softening judgment, and meeting imperfection without needing to sort everything out. The practice gradually reframes thought as an old, innocent survival response and opens into a more welcoming relationship with silence.
short_summary: A guided meditation that treats thinking as a coping response and invites a softer, more accepting relationship with experience.

chapters.title: Lineage and trust
chapters.description: The meditation begins by situating practice in a long lineage of wisdom and love, encouraging trust in what has already been carried forward.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: He invites listeners to stop inventing wisdom, let the body settle, and take cues from the momentum of the Dharma.

chapters.title: Blessing imperfection
chapters.description: The practice turns toward making peace with imperfection and loosening the habit of sorting life into right and wrong.

chapters.title: Safety and urgency
chapters.description: Matthew explores how feeling unsafe makes thinking more sticky, then uses body relaxation as a way of reassuring the system enough to let go.

chapters.title: Thinking as coping
chapters.description: He reframes thought as a coping strategy for samsara and encourages simple, non-elaborative mindfulness of pleasant and unpleasant experience.

chapters.title: Peace with imperfection
chapters.description: The meditation deepens around reducing compulsive safety behaviors and making peace with what is incomplete or imperfect.

chapters.title: Innocent thinking
chapters.description: He softens judgment of the mind by describing thinking as something conditioned and ancient, not something to blame.

chapters.title: Silence welcomes you
chapters.description: The closing movement invites rest in silence, with a sense of being received just as you are.

title: Love & Rest Daylong - Tranquility, Shinzen Young's Do-Nothing Practice, Intro and Guided
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver explores tranquility as a necessary form of protection, not avoidance, and frames the path as a balance between seclusion and opening. He then moves through concrete ways of settling the body and senses—breath, gravity, silence, visual blankness—before introducing Shinzen Young’s do-nothing practice as a rest from controlling attention. The talk gradually turns from skillful stabilization into a guided period of letting experience be, including the release of self-correction and the recognition that there is often nothing to improve in the moment.
short_summary: A talk on tranquility as refuge, followed by a guided introduction to Shinzen Young’s do-nothing practice.

chapters.title: Tranquility and insight
chapters.description: Matthew opens with the Buddha’s teaching that tranquility and insight arise together, then explains tranquility as a protective settling of the heart rather than avoidance.

chapters.title: Protection and sensitivity
chapters.description: He describes the dialectic between opening and seclusion, and how practice must honor individual sensitivity, brittleness, and overwhelm.

chapters.title: Why settling matters
chapters.description: The talk turns to the need for tranquilizing the mind before meeting grief, fear, and sensory intensity, using attention as something that shapes experience deeply.

chapters.title: Breath and body
chapters.description: Drawing on Shinzen Young and other teachers, Matthew offers ways to settle through the breath, gravity, elemental heaviness, and the body’s sense of relaxation.

chapters.title: Listening to silence
chapters.description: He guides attention toward silence inside and outside, and toward the visual field as blankness rather than as a set of objects.

chapters.title: Do-nothing practice
chapters.description: Matthew introduces Shinzen Young’s do-nothing instructions, emphasizing the dropping of any intention to control attention and the possibility of deep rest.

chapters.title: Guided release
chapters.description: A long guided practice follows, beginning with fuller breathing and grounding, then moving into silence, visual rest, and surrender of effort and preference.

chapters.title: No good or bad meditation
chapters.description: The practice deepens into letting whatever happens happen, with repeated reminders to drop self-correction, release the urge to engineer experience, and leave oneself alone.

title: Guided Meditation: Stabilizing; Compassion
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that begins with settling the body in a comfortable, alert posture and gently questioning the habit of vigilance. The practice moves into sensing breath throughout the body, especially neglected or tense areas, before opening into compassion for one’s own pain. He emphasizes a patient, steady love that does not try to erase suffering, but meets it with care and presence.
short_summary: A guided meditation that stabilizes attention in the body and then turns toward compassionate care for suffering.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: He invites a comfortable, even cozy setup and a posture that is both relaxed and alert.

chapters.title: Letting Go of Vigilance
chapters.description: The meditation explores how worry and clinging can feel protective, then encourages permission to relax.

chapters.title: Kind Intention
chapters.description: Brief loving phrases begin to establish a gentle, supportive tone.

chapters.title: Breath in the Body
chapters.description: Attention widens to the breath filling the body and sensing breath in the back of the body.

chapters.title: Unifying Numb Areas
chapters.description: He guides the breath into zones of numbness, flatness, and neglected sensation.

chapters.title: Soothing Intensity
chapters.description: The practice includes cold spots, heat, and hyper-aroused energy, letting the breath steady and unify the body.

chapters.title: Turning Toward Compassion
chapters.description: With the body more settled, the meditation shifts to tenderly meeting one’s own sorrow, grief, rage, or fear.

chapters.title: Words of Care
chapters.description: He offers phrases such as caring about pain and asking for freedom from suffering, linking personal pain to wider compassion.

chapters.title: Patient Love
chapters.description: The close emphasizes compassion as a patient presence that does not need to make pain disappear.

title: Guided Meditation: In the Presence of Love (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by settling the body with fuller breaths and a felt sense of love, then repeatedly returns to gravity, breath, and the present moment. Matthew Brensilver gently explores how hidden agendas, self-fixing, and projection can create tension, and he invites a more direct trust in awareness that is not transactional. The talk closes by widening practice into daily life, with an emphasis on tenderness, walking, and letting wisdom and love meet the human condition.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing into the present moment and meeting experience with awareness, tenderness, and love.

chapters.title: Settling into breath
chapters.description: The practice opens by softening the breath and sensing a wave of awareness and love across the body.

chapters.title: Giving over to gravity
chapters.description: Brensilver invites surrender to gravity and Dharma, gradually orienting the body and heart toward ease.

chapters.title: Hidden agendas
chapters.description: He names subtle agendas to avoid pain, improve practice, or control experience, and shows how they interfere with contact.

chapters.title: Nothing to fix
chapters.description: The talk turns toward the idea that the defective self is not something to manage, but a symptom of deeper pain.

chapters.title: Nontransactional awareness
chapters.description: Awareness is presented as something that does not bargain for results, but rests simply in the present.

chapters.title: Letting reference points go
chapters.description: He explores how projection and craving sustain a sense of continuity, then asks the listener to relax fixed points of identity.

chapters.title: Falling into love
chapters.description: The practice deepens into surrender, with faith that love will catch what is released into the present moment.

chapters.title: Love in daily life
chapters.description: In the closing reflections, he speaks about tears, poignancy, wisdom, and bringing the softness of practice into walking and everyday tasks.

title: Guided Meditation: Relax into Alertness
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on relaxing without losing clarity, beginning with a sense of sangha and settling into the body. He repeatedly reframes ease as a way of becoming more vivid, more attentive, and more genuinely alert, rather than drifting into dullness. The practice moves from balancing tiredness and arousal to resting the heart, noticing the mind’s states, and letting awareness do some of the work.
short_summary: A guided meditation on softening effort so attention can become bright, steady, and receptive.

chapters.title: Entering Sangha
chapters.description: The meditation begins by sensing the presence and good will of other beings, then turning toward the body-mind.

chapters.title: Balancing Energy
chapters.description: He invites a check-in with tiredness, alertness, and the felt sense of balance, without forcing the experience.

chapters.title: Relaxing for Clarity
chapters.description: Relaxation is framed as a way to know more, feel more, and become genuinely alert rather than vague.

chapters.title: Gentle Single-Pointing
chapters.description: Attention is described as poised and selective, able to soak into one object while the rest of experience stays in the background.

chapters.title: Letting Go of Worry
chapters.description: He names the habit of scanning for threat and opportunity, then encourages simplicity instead of compulsive thinking.

chapters.title: Putting Down Busyness
chapters.description: The practice opens to the burdens of life while also giving the heart rest so they can be met more lovingly.

chapters.title: Not Missing Goodness
chapters.description: The emphasis shifts to the goodness already present, which does not need to be engineered or strived toward.

chapters.title: Watching the Mind
chapters.description: Attention turns from the breath to the mind itself, noticing brightness, dullness, agitation, tranquility, grasping, aversion, and peace.

chapters.title: Stability as Object
chapters.description: If some steadiness has developed, stability itself can become the object, opening into a more grounded and open awareness.

title: Guided Meditation: Gentleness & Equanimity
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how gentleness and equanimity can work together: honest about what is here, and non-violent toward experience. The practice moves from settling into silence and breath, through softening the impulse to control, toward a deeper trust in allowing pleasant and unpleasant to come and go. Near the end, he points to a quiet contrast between clinging and love, and suggests that not trying to do anything with the mind may be the most gentle move of all.
short_summary: A guided meditation on meeting experience with radical honesty, gentleness, and equanimity.

chapters.title: Held by Silence
chapters.description: The meditation begins by settling into silence and breathing, with an invitation to feel held even amid noise.

chapters.title: Radical Honesty
chapters.description: He frames the practice as refusing pretence and meeting experience with clear, honest attention.

chapters.title: Not Fighting to Heal
chapters.description: The teaching turns toward trust: healing and waking up do not require force, but a patient softening over time.

chapters.title: Gentle Strength
chapters.description: Gentleness is presented as compatible with courage and strength, with awareness blessing its objects rather than grasping them.

chapters.title: Connecting, Not Controlling
chapters.description: He contrasts control with connection and invites staying present with ambivalence instead of pushing experience away.

chapters.title: Hands Soft, Clear
chapters.description: Using the image of washing a baby's head, he describes how to hold experience with tenderness, clarity, and surrender to pleasant and unpleasant states.

chapters.title: Knowing the Mind
chapters.description: The practice deepens into the insight that the mind is known best when we stop trying to manipulate it.

chapters.title: Clinging and Love
chapters.description: The close turns quiet and spare, distinguishing busy clinging from still love and ending with a simple blessing of shared sitting.

title: Guided Meditation: Tranquility
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation oriented toward settling the body into tranquility without losing aliveness. The practice moves from posture and breath into the belly, then into working gently with hyper-arousal, urgency, and scattered attention. Later he invites resting with whatever pleasantness appears so that ease and tranquility can reinforce one another.
short_summary: A guided practice for calming the body, softening urgency, and building tranquil vitality.

chapters.title: Settling in
chapters.description: He begins by orienting the body and naming how to work with sleepiness or restlessness.

chapters.title: Posture and gravity
chapters.description: The guidance turns to posture, giving the body to gravity and letting the spine rise gently.

chapters.title: Dropping to the belly
chapters.description: He invites attention down from the head into the belly and explores a softer center of gravity.

chapters.title: Softening the headspace
chapters.description: The practice emphasizes releasing tension and noticing the perspective that seems to live in the head.

chapters.title: Working with arousal
chapters.description: He names the body’s hyper-arousal and urges the listener to meet active zones with breath and awareness.

chapters.title: Urgency and false alarms
chapters.description: The talk reframes everyday agitation as something to acknowledge without automatically obeying.

chapters.title: Accountability without blame
chapters.description: He encourages radical but blameless attention to what body and mind are doing moment by moment.

chapters.title: Following pleasantness
chapters.description: The meditation shifts toward any thread of comfort, inviting the listener to rest in it and let tranquility grow.

title: Guided Meditation: Concentration, Clarity, Equanimity
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed by gratitude for practice, then builds the session around three qualities of attention: concentration, clarity, and equanimity. He uses simple images and practical instructions to show how attention can stabilize, sharpen, and become less reactive to pleasure, pain, and change. The meditation gradually turns toward a more open stance: meeting each moment without landing on conclusions, reference points, or grasping.
short_summary: A guided meditation on stabilizing attention, seeing experience more clearly, and relaxing reactivity through equanimity.

chapters.title: Opening Gratitude
chapters.description: The meditation begins with gratitude for having a path and letting that gratitude soften the heart.

chapters.title: What Attention Needs
chapters.description: Matthew shifts from the object of attention to the qualities of attention, introducing concentration as the first part of the practice.

chapters.title: Higher-Resolution Clarity
chapters.description: He describes clarity as perceiving experience with greater resolution, both in time and in the detail of the object.

chapters.title: Equanimity and Frictionlessness
chapters.description: The talk turns to equanimity as becoming less reactive to changing conditions, while preferences can remain.

chapters.title: Practice Moment by Moment
chapters.description: He returns to direct instruction on choosing an anchor and cultivating stability, clarity, and equanimity despite distraction.

chapters.title: Rehearsing Freedom
chapters.description: The instruction becomes more encouraging, presenting each moment as a chance to rehearse freedom and trust the path.

chapters.title: Open-Ended Release
chapters.description: The closing points toward not landing the mind anywhere, then ends with a Rumi line that supports non-clinging and spaciousness.

title: Guided Meditation: Effort as Not-Self
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on effort as a kind of not-self practice: instead of pushing harder, the instruction is to stop what can be stopped and receive the breath more openly. The talk explores how meditation can crystallize a sense of a separate doer, and then gently redirects attention toward a deeper, less anxious source of practice. After the instruction, the guidance settles into extended periods of silence punctuated by reminders to be seated and to avoid harsh, moralistic self-talk.
short_summary: A guided meditation on softening effort, loosening the sense of the meditator, and receiving the breath with less selfing.

chapters.title: Settling and Stopping
chapters.description: The opening invites a first minute of doing nothing and points toward noticing how much can simply be stopped.

chapters.title: Freedom Already Here
chapters.description: The guidance frames meditation as trusting that goodness and freedom are already present, rather than being forced into being by self-effort.

chapters.title: How Effort Becomes Self
chapters.description: Brensilver explains how intention and attention can harden into a sense of an inner meditator pulling the strings.

chapters.title: Relaxing the Impulse
chapters.description: The practice turns toward relaxing the urge to do and learning to trust what is less forced and more receptive.

chapters.title: Receiving the Breath
chapters.description: A longer stretch of silent practice emphasizes a different mode of attention: openly receiving the breath without congealing a separate self around it.

chapters.title: Choosing the Right Counsel
chapters.description: The closing instruction cautions against impatient or harsh inner voices and encourages drawing practice direction from something deeper, more embodied, and less compulsive.

title: Guided Meditation: Intimacy with Breathing
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation stays close to the breath while exploring how attention can become more intimate with sensation. Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to notice the breath as fresh, immediate, and sensed through the whole body rather than held as an abstract object. The practice also makes room for tiredness, loose ends, and sticky emotions, returning again and again to breathing as a simple home base.
short_summary: A guided meditation on settling into the breath as a fresh, intimate, and reliable home for attention.

chapters.title: Arriving With Breath
chapters.description: The meditation opens by orienting attention to the familiar breath and its fresh quality.

chapters.title: Images and Sensation
chapters.description: He explores how subtle visual imagery can guide attention into the felt sensations of breathing.

chapters.title: Thin Slices of Time
chapters.description: The instruction turns toward intimacy with breathing and the question of how to meet each moment more closely.

chapters.title: Breath Awakens the Senses
chapters.description: Breathing is described as something that can enliven the senses and meet us even when we are tired.

chapters.title: Peace and Refuge
chapters.description: The talk distinguishes real peace from false refuge and invites making peace with the loose ends of life.

chapters.title: Forgiving the Mind
chapters.description: He acknowledges the mind's patrolling and foraging, then points back toward reconnection with this moment and the Dharma.

chapters.title: Home in the Breath
chapters.description: The practice settles into simplicity, with the breath as a place where experience becomes small, clear, and enough.

chapters.title: Sticky Thoughts to Breath
chapters.description: He suggests meeting sticky emotional activation in the body first, then returning to the rhythm of breathing when it softens.

title: Guided Meditation: Breath, Pleasure, Opening
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with a gentle invitation to soften the face and notice a simple, almost hidden smile. From there, Matthew Brensilver develops a practice of allowing pleasure to be present alongside difficulty, using the breath as a place to meet aliveness without forcing anything to disappear. The talk gradually widens into open awareness, then loosens even the instructions themselves so the practice can rest in sound, sensation, and trust.
short_summary: A guided meditation on softening, noticing subtle pleasure, and opening awareness without pushing away difficulty.

chapters.title: Settling with a Smile
chapters.description: The meditation begins by inviting a barely noticeable smile and letting that ease spread through the face and body.

chapters.title: Joy with Difficulty
chapters.description: He frames the practice as making room for joy without denying suffering, especially the mixed and ambiguous nature of experience.

chapters.title: Relaxing and Receiving
chapters.description: Attention is guided to release tension where possible and rest on something soothing or pleasantly subtle in the background.

chapters.title: Breath and Pleasure
chapters.description: Pleasant feeling is invited to mingle with breathing, with the breath understood as a living rhythm of receiving and release.

chapters.title: An Anchor Everywhere
chapters.description: The practice shifts from a narrow breath anchor toward a wider field in which awareness includes everything.

chapters.title: Opening to Awareness
chapters.description: He emphasizes waking up to arising and passing experience without building a fixed viewpoint, ending in trust in awareness itself.

chapters.title: Letting Instructions Go
chapters.description: The close loosens the guidance itself, noting the pull to make meaning and inviting the listener to let voice and instruction become simple sound.

title: Guided Meditation: Not Predicting Me
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around softening the habit of predicting who we are from one moment to the next. He points to the “song of self” as something that can be noticed rather than believed, and uses that noticing to make breathing and body awareness feel less crowded. The practice gradually turns toward a quieter, more global wakefulness in which experience is allowed to be wider than any fixed story.
short_summary: A guided meditation on releasing self-prediction and resting in a wider, less crowded awareness.

chapters.title: Arrival
chapters.description: A brief welcome and invitation to practice together without a fixed plan.

chapters.title: Predicting Ourselves
chapters.description: He explores how the mind keeps rehearsing a model of self from moment to moment.

chapters.title: Opening to Not-Knowing
chapters.description: The talk widens into questions about what it would be like not to expect the next moment to feel like the same person.

chapters.title: The Song of Self
chapters.description: He names the recurring self-story as something that can be heard, noticed, and slightly alienated from.

chapters.title: Love It to Death
chapters.description: Using a phrase attributed to Shinzen Young, he suggests meeting selfing with enough familiarity that it loses its grip.

chapters.title: Settling Into Breath
chapters.description: The practice turns toward surrendering into breathing as expectations and moral pressure begin to loosen.

chapters.title: Global Wakefulness
chapters.description: In the closing instructions, he emphasizes that no story can be a lasting home and invites a wider wakefulness to all of life.

title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing into the Moment
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on relaxing into the moment with honesty rather than trying to fix experience. The practice moves from settling the breath and body to meeting thoughts and worries with less struggle, then widens into a steadier sense of refuge and trust. By the end, the emphasis is on honoring the moment as a form of healing in itself.
short_summary: A guided meditation that uses the breath, body awareness, and trust to soften the habit of solving the moment and rest in what is here.

chapters.title: Honesty With the Moment
chapters.description: The meditation opens by framing practice as a truthful meeting with experience, rather than pretending or controlling it.

chapters.title: Settling With Breath
chapters.description: Instruction turns to fuller breathing and repeated reassurance that it is safe to be here, just as things are.

chapters.title: Relaxing the Problem Habit
chapters.description: The talk loosens the urge to treat the present as something to solve and encourages a softer trust in whatever is arising.

chapters.title: Body as Refuge
chapters.description: Awareness expands through the front, back, and lower body, using the breath to animate and unify the whole body.

chapters.title: Soothing and Energizing
chapters.description: Breath is presented as a way to enliven what is numb and calm what is overactivated, bringing the body into greater balance.

chapters.title: Dissolving Worry
chapters.description: The guidance contrasts thinking with breathing awareness, suggesting that worries can loosen when met from tranquility and surrender.

chapters.title: Finding Refuge
chapters.description: Attention returns to the most vivid place of breathing and to a sense that thought is not the only important thing in experience.

chapters.title: What Truly Matters
chapters.description: The meditation closes by reflecting that honoring the moment leaves a durable imprint, refining attention and resting the heart from struggle.

title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing into Love
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by orienting the body toward ease without losing wakefulness, then moves into breath, heart center, and a softening toward love. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the idea that when the mind and body settle, awareness can take on the hue of kindness, and he invites a willingness to feel whatever is present—joy, sorrow, pain, or tenderness—without contracting around it. The practice widens into phrases such as “May I be well” and “May all beings be well,” ending in a quiet emphasis on non-violence, refuge, and benevolent wish.
short_summary: A guided practice of settling the body, softening the heart, and widening into lovingkindness for self and all beings.

chapters.title: Settling and posture
chapters.description: He begins by inviting relaxation balanced with an upright spine and a wakeful sitting posture.

chapters.title: Breath as softening
chapters.description: The attention moves to fuller breaths and filling out the body so relaxation becomes more embodied.

chapters.title: Awareness with love
chapters.description: He links settling with sensitivity to love and asks for a heart-mind that offers itself to the moment without contract.

chapters.title: Heart posture
chapters.description: The practice deepens into trusting that whatever arises can become love, with attention placed in the heart center.

chapters.title: Bathing pain in care
chapters.description: He acknowledges knots and pain in life and encourages meeting them with care, kindness, and an image or phrase that steadies the breath.

chapters.title: Lovingkindness phrases
chapters.description: The meditation shifts into explicit phrases such as “May I be well,” “May my heart be as wide as the world,” and “May I take refuge in love.”},{

title: Saturday Guided Sit
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided sit centered on feeling-tone: noticing the body, relaxing the impulse to do more, and sensing how arousal and valence shape experience. He repeatedly returns to receiving experience rather than building an action plan from it, and later connects that to the Buddha’s teaching that all dhammas converge on feeling. The closing widens into practical retreat advice about silence, habit, and staying close to practice as the day unfolds.
short_summary: A guided sit on feeling-tone, embodied awareness, and meeting experience without turning it into a story or plan.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: He begins by inviting attention to the body and the heart’s inner posture, then encourages less doing and more careful stopping.

chapters.title: Whole-body awareness
chapters.description: The guidance turns toward sensing the whole body, noticing what is often missed, and feeling the body’s edges and density in space.

chapters.title: Arousal and valence
chapters.description: He introduces arousal and valence as key dimensions of experience, including how emotions and simple sensations are colored by them.

chapters.title: Clarifying feeling
chapters.description: The practice focuses on mapping feeling in the body and recognizing that pleasant, unpleasant, and neutral states all arise within the same field.

chapters.title: Receiving without counsel
chapters.description: Rather than using feeling to make decisions, he invites being softened by experience and repeatedly returns to freedom as available in each moment.

chapters.title: The self-story loosens
chapters.description: He describes how affect can seem to issue orders and create a sense of self, then shows how awareness and disidentification make that grip easier to release.

chapters.title: Feeling as practice
chapters.description: The talk names the Buddha’s teaching that all dhammas converge on feeling and uses it to frame feeling as a field to honor, not always interpret.

chapters.title: Retreat and closing notes
chapters.description: He ends with practical reminders about staying held by the path, noticing habits, protecting silence, and meeting the rest of the retreat with sincerity and openness.

title: Thursday Guided Meditation
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by normalizing fear, bracing, and doubt, inviting them to be met with kindness rather than treated as a problem. It then settles into breath as an anchor, widening into the body as a whole, and later turns toward the way attention drifts into memory, anticipation, and the impulse to fix things. The practice closes with a softer sense of surrender and a hint of how intentions can line up when effort becomes less forceful.
short_summary: A guided meditation on meeting fear kindly, settling with the breath, and loosening the mind’s habit of fixing and controlling.

chapters.title: Arriving with fear
chapters.description: The meditation begins by noticing fear, bracing, and the unease that can come with starting practice.

chapters.title: Common practice difficulties
chapters.description: Restlessness, sleepiness, doubt, and aversion are named as familiar conditions rather than signs of failure, with a brief reference to the Buddha.

chapters.title: Finding the breath
chapters.description: Attention is gathered on the breath at a chosen point in the body, with fuller breathing used to settle and clarify.

chapters.title: Breath through the body
chapters.description: The breath is explored as something that can soothe agitation, enliven numbness, and be felt through the whole body rather than only at one spot.

chapters.title: Willingness and curiosity
chapters.description: The instruction shifts from technique toward an attitude of willingness, curiosity, and interest in what attention and awareness are doing.

chapters.title: Grounded openness
chapters.description: The body is sensed as open yet grounded, with gravity and the belly becoming a place of stability.

chapters.title: Memory and anticipation
chapters.description: The mind’s movement into memory and future planning is described as innocent, along with the habit of using thought to stabilize experience.

chapters.title: Letting intentions align
chapters.description: The closing reflection notices background intentions, control, and aversion, then suggests that when we let go, the directions can come into alignment.

title: Formal Opening Part 1 (sit)
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: A brief opening for retreat, centered on beginning with a 15-minute sit and then moving through logistics for the days ahead. Matthew Brensilver covers seating, hearing assistance, the basic alternation of sitting and walking meditation, and the schedule for instruction, talks, reflections, and discussion groups. The talk ends by naming the value of everyone having set aside time for retreat.
short_summary: A short retreat opening with instructions, logistics, and the basic rhythm of the week.

chapters.title: Opening the retreat
chapters.description: The retreat begins with a short invitation to sit and bless the time together through practice.

chapters.title: Comfort and seating
chapters.description: Practical guidance on hearing devices, cushions, chairs, and making the body more workable for practice.

chapters.title: Daily meditation rhythm
chapters.description: The basic rhythm is outlined: alternating silent sitting and walking meditation through the day.

chapters.title: Instruction and talks
chapters.description: He names the main touchpoints in the schedule, including morning instruction, the afternoon Dharma talk, and the evening reflection.

chapters.title: Practice groups
chapters.description: Participants are told how the discussion groups will work and that they will meet with both teachers over the course of the retreat.

chapters.title: Bell practice
chapters.description: Those ringing the bells are invited to practice beforehand, with a light touch preferred.

chapters.title: Closing encouragement
chapters.description: The opening closes with a check for questions and a simple appreciation for the time everyone has set aside.

title: Guided Meditation: Feeling and Verbal Thinking
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation opens by questioning the impulse to reach for technique and inviting a more undefended, surrendering posture. From there, Matthew Brensilver leads attention into bodily feeling, emotional charge, and the way inner speech can be heard as sound rather than obeyed automatically. The meditation gradually widens into a quiet, patient awareness of the relationship between feelings and words, ending with an emphasis on allowing experience to unfold with blessing and equanimity.
short_summary: A guided meditation that explores bodily feeling, inner speech, and the practice of meeting both with patient awareness.

chapters.title: Arriving and Settling
chapters.description: Matthew welcomes the group and begins by inviting a simple sitting practice.

chapters.title: Letting Go of Technique
chapters.description: He questions the urge to use technique as coping and asks what it is like to sit undefended.

chapters.title: Breath and Release
chapters.description: The practice shifts into fuller breathing and a sense of relaxing and cleansing the body.

chapters.title: Feeling in the Body
chapters.description: Attention turns to emotional and reactive sensations in the body, with emphasis on their location, intensity, and changing quality.

chapters.title: Patience and Permission
chapters.description: He encourages bathing feeling states in attention and patience, allowing them to move and self-liberate in their own time.

chapters.title: Hearing Thoughts
chapters.description: The meditation explores how thinking can be noticed as auditory experience, using Shinzen Young's teaching as support.

chapters.title: Feelings and Stories
chapters.description: He brings together words and feelings, showing how emotion can prompt stories and explanations that seem to stabilize experience.

chapters.title: Equanimity and Blessing
chapters.description: The closing invites equanimity with both bodily urgency and the mind's narration, ending in awareness, patience, and love.

title: Guided Meditation: You cannot know oneself while fixing oneself
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on resting rather than fixing, beginning with posture, grounding, and the breath as a way of settling the body-mind. The practice gradually opens into a quiet inquiry into samsara, fear, and the urge to make meaning, with repeated reminders that what arises can be met without willfulness. Near the end, the instruction turns toward simple attentiveness: silence as anchor, and even noise met with infinite patience.
short_summary: A guided meditation on grounding, surrendering the fixing habit, and meeting experience with patience rather than willfulness.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: The meditation begins with posture, grounding, and a gentle sense of the spine and breath.

chapters.title: Breath and ease
chapters.description: Breathing is used to smooth the body-mind and create a steadier field of attention.

chapters.title: Putting down the project
chapters.description: Brensilver invites listeners to stop trying to fix life and to trust that help does not need to be hunted down.

chapters.title: Simple, beckoning practice
chapters.description: The talk narrows to the simplicity of body and breath, with a repeated sense that practice does not require willfulness.

chapters.title: Phenomena and fear
chapters.description: As thoughts and fears arise, the instruction turns to meeting them without freezing time or needing immediate reassurance.

chapters.title: Wisp-like experience
chapters.description: Experience is described as vivid yet attenuated, and the key line appears that if we only try to fix ourselves, we will never know ourselves.

chapters.title: Silence and patience
chapters.description: The closing instruction names silence as anchor and asks that every bit of noise be met with infinite patience.

title: Guided Meditation: Pervasiveness of Craving
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on craving, control, and the tendency to shape experience. The practice moves from settling the body into a posture of surrender, to noticing the tension of holding on, to softening around heartache and the wish for life to be different. He repeatedly returns to the possibility that awareness can be trusted, and that this moment can be received as a source of wisdom rather than a problem to solve.
short_summary: A guided meditation on recognizing craving and easing the habit of control through surrender, breath, and trust in awareness.

chapters.title: Settling Into Posture
chapters.description: The practice begins with posture, breath, and the balance between alertness and tranquility.

chapters.title: Noticing Holding On
chapters.description: He names craving, clinging, and the tension created by trying to control experience.

chapters.title: Surrendering to This Moment
chapters.description: The guidance turns toward accepting imperfection, letting go of self-shaping, and relaxing.

chapters.title: Breath as Pleasure
chapters.description: Attention to breathing is reframed as a simple pleasure rather than a task or test.

chapters.title: Responsibility Without Power
chapters.description: He reflects on the anxiety of feeling responsible for life while not fully in control of it.

chapters.title: Trusting Awareness
chapters.description: The meditation asks whether awareness can be trusted, while allowing coping without self-judgment.

chapters.title: Receiving Wisdom
chapters.description: In the closing stretch, the repeated invitation is to let this moment be received and trusted as wisdom.

title: Guided Meditation: Too Busy Knowing
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: A guided meditation built around relaxing vigilance and trusting the body’s capacity to meet experience. Matthew Brensilver invites a quieter mode of attention: less patrol, more surrender, while keeping the mind clear and awake. The practice returns several times to impermanence and to the felt sense of experience changing moment by moment, without freezing it into something solid.
short_summary: A guided meditation on softening vigilance, meeting experience directly, and knowing impermanence without clinging.

chapters.title: Settling and Relaxing
chapters.description: The practice begins by relaxing whatever can be relaxed and noticing that softening is not about forcing anything away.

chapters.title: Safety and Relinquishment
chapters.description: He invites a temporary assumption of safety so vigilance can ease and the mind can surrender to what is present.

chapters.title: Anchors and Clarity
chapters.description: Breath, sound, the hands, or bodily pleasantness can serve as anchors while attention remains clear rather than foggy.

chapters.title: Snagged by Experience
chapters.description: Attention catches on sensations and phenomena, like fabric snagging on a hangnail, and the practice notices that snagging without panic.

chapters.title: Moment by Moment Knowing
chapters.description: Impermanence is brought forward as the mind learns to know experience in motion instead of holding it still.

chapters.title: Too Busy Knowing
chapters.description: The talk ends on the idea that intimate knowing can be so continuous that it leaves little room for a fixed identity.

title: Guided Meditation: Sitting in the Bones
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on settling attention into the body, especially the felt sense of the bones, heaviness, and gravity. The practice moves from fuller breathing and relaxation into a steadier, mountain-like groundedness, then broadens into the freedom to let experience arise and pass without strain. In the closing, he brings in loving-kindness as a quiet ethical and emotional dimension of the practice.
short_summary: A guided meditation on resting attention in the bones, releasing effort, and letting steadiness open into metta.

chapters.title: Settling the Breath
chapters.description: Fuller breathing is used to soften the belly and signal the body that it can relax.

chapters.title: Sitting in the Bones
chapters.description: The instruction turns to feeling the body as grounded and heavy, with attention dropping below the head.

chapters.title: Gravity and Earth
chapters.description: He emphasizes the downward pull of gravity and the sense of the earth offering support.

chapters.title: Attention Downward
chapters.description: When awareness drifts upward, the instruction is to pour it back into the body and let it seem to arise from the belly.

chapters.title: Mountain Stability
chapters.description: The practice is framed as growing rootedness, with a brief reference to Chögyam Trungpa's mountain image.

chapters.title: Stop Doing So Much
chapters.description: Relaxation is presented as a release from strain and self-effort, trusting awareness to remain awake without constant doing.

chapters.title: The Self Can Relax
chapters.description: The sense of a contracted 'me' is invited to loosen, making room for a wider, less possessive awareness.

chapters.title: From Rest to Metta
chapters.description: Steadiness is linked with love, and the talk ends by folding loving-kindness into the practice as something we may need even when we do not feel inclined toward it.

title: Guided Meditation: Landing in the Breath and Soundscape
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
description: (Big Bear Retreat Center) These recordings are available publicly from a retreat held in person at Big Bear Retreat Center. Learn more about the center and upcoming offerings for retreats in nature, gathered in community. bigbearretreatcenter.org/upcoming-retreats/ Healing the Self, Loving the Self, Forgetting the Self Matthew Brensilver July 9 - 13, 2024 Co-sponsored by Insight Retreat Center (IRC) and Big Bear Retreat Center In this silent retreat, we explore how mindfulness supports the healing of the self. Many of the Buddhist teachings help us to tend to painful memory, old pains and the habits that compound suffering. As we become more gentle and loving towards experience, the self becomes less and less of a preoccupation. The more completely we accept ourselves, the easier it becomes to forget the self and rest in an awareness unencumbered by self-consciousness. This retreat includes sitting and walking meditation instructions, and dharma talks.
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation and retreat orientation that begins with fear, softness, and the need to feel safe enough to simplify attention. He then moves through breath practice, soundscape awareness, and walking meditation, returning again and again to the idea that practice is less about willpower than willingness. Near the end, he frames boredom, overwhelm, and even ordinary retreat difficulty as part of the field to be known with gentleness.
short_summary: A guided meditation on settling fear, resting in breath and sound, and carrying gentle awareness into walking and the rest of retreat.

chapters.title: Settling fear
chapters.description: He opens by naming fear as a deep shaping force and invites kindness toward any bracing or anxiety present.

chapters.title: Mood in awareness
chapters.description: The instruction turns to seeing background mood directly, letting it be held in awareness rather than filtering experience through it.

chapters.title: Breath as refuge
chapters.description: He guides attention to the breath in a precise but relaxed way, emphasizing release at the end of the exhale and trust in the body.

chapters.title: Thinking without struggle
chapters.description: Matthew explores the mind’s habit of strategizing and securing itself through thought, and suggests using awareness instead of words for refuge.

chapters.title: Awake and relaxed
chapters.description: He softens the approach further, contrasting alertness with defensiveness and inviting a simple, steady attention.

chapters.title: Listening to soundscape
chapters.description: For those for whom the breath is not enough, he offers sound and silence as another anchor, including nearby, distant, and ending sounds.

chapters.title: Retreat rhythm
chapters.description: The talk shifts into practical retreat guidance about how the first day can feel swampy, why it helps to normalize difficulty, and how sitting and walking support each other.

chapters.title: Whole-day awareness
chapters.description: He draws on Sayadaw Tejaniya and a rotating set of anchors to encourage broad, continuous awareness, including boredom, simple sensation, and the possibility that practice changes us quietly over time.

chapters.title: Closing encouragement
chapters.description: Matthew closes with practical reminders about pacing, practice meetings, and letting both ease and discomfort soften the heart.

title: Guided Meditation: Non-tension
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on non-tension: noticing how the body and mind respond to conditions, and learning not to wait for things to be perfect before letting go. The practice moves from settling into bodily awareness, to relaxing clinging and bracing, to resting with impermanence without pushing or pulling against it. The closing section uses a repeated image of falling into the present, with less baggage and fewer places for attention to snag.
short_summary: A guided meditation on releasing tension by meeting conditions as they are and relaxing into impermanence.

chapters.title: Opening and welcome
chapters.description: Matthew welcomes listeners and introduces the practice with a light, informal tone.

chapters.title: Temperature and the body
chapters.description: He recalls a teacher’s reminder that even the Buddha sweats and shivers, using discomfort to point toward direct observation.

chapters.title: Not waiting for perfect
chapters.description: The talk turns to letting imperfection become motivation to stop fighting and to give permission for the moment to be as it is.

chapters.title: Awareness in the body
chapters.description: Listeners are guided to fill the body with awareness in a way that feels expansive rather than forced.

chapters.title: Relaxing clinging
chapters.description: He names clinging as a source of tension and invites a settled, undefended aliveness.

chapters.title: Impermanence as practice
chapters.description: Matthew links attention with change and encourages not pushing or pulling on impermanence or seeking escape from it.

chapters.title: Falling into presence
chapters.description: The closing refrain uses the image of falling deeper into the present, with less baggage and fewer ways for attention to catch.

title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing and Falling Awake
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on settling the body and mind without forcing ease. The practice moves from posture and releasing the day, through soft attention to breath and sound, toward a gentler understanding of relaxation as relinquishment rather than control. He returns repeatedly to the idea that tension is bound up with grasping, and that waking up may happen in a way closer to falling asleep than to trying harder.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing effort, softening control, and letting the mind fall awake.

chapters.title: Settling Into Posture
chapters.description: He begins by inviting a balanced posture that tilts toward alertness or relaxation depending on what is needed.

chapters.title: Laying Down the Day
chapters.description: The guidance turns toward whatever is still entangling from the day or week, with encouragement to feel and bless it without getting more caught.

chapters.title: Blessing and Release
chapters.description: He emphasizes giving blessing to what is present so it can be set down rather than carried forward.

chapters.title: Breath and Sound
chapters.description: Attention opens to the ends of exhalations and to very faint sounds, including the edge between hearing and silence.

chapters.title: Tension and Holding
chapters.description: The talk names greed, hatred, selfing, and duality as forms of tension, and contrasts them with the limits of willful relaxation.

chapters.title: Relinquishing Control
chapters.description: He describes a faith-filled softening in which the mind stops trying to engineer experience and lets the moment do some of the work.

chapters.title: Relaxing as Exhale
chapters.description: The meditation continues with a quieter settling around the felt sense of relaxing, leading into a brief reflective turn on the practice itself.

chapters.title: Falling Awake
chapters.description: He closes by contrasting commanded relaxation with genuine relinquishment, comparing it to falling asleep and suggesting that awakening can happen from openness.

title: Guided Meditation: Relaxation and the Tension of Dukkha, Self, Clinging
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that uses relaxation as a way to notice the subtle tension bound up with clinging, selfing, greed, hatred, and delusion. He moves from an image of a sleepy child asking for comfort to a quieter investigation of how deep release can reveal what the body-mind has been holding. The practice is gentle but pointed: soften, breathe, let gravity help, and keep putting things down until awareness feels less burdened.
short_summary: A guided meditation on deep relaxation as a way to meet the tension of clinging, selfing, and suffering.

chapters.title: Opening story
chapters.description: Matthew begins with a family story that turns into a playful question about where awareness and the Dharma are found.

chapters.title: Relaxation and suffering
chapters.description: He introduces the idea of awakening through deep relaxation and names tension as a signature of greed, hatred, selfing, and suffering.

chapters.title: Right here to sit
chapters.description: The talk turns toward the immediacy of practice, with the reminder that the relief we seek may be close at hand.

chapters.title: Let tension speak
chapters.description: He suggests allowing tension permission to be present, while using breath and awareness to invite ease rather than struggle.

chapters.title: Gravity and ease
chapters.description: The guidance shifts into body-based support, especially the soothing downward pull of gravity and an upright but released posture.

chapters.title: Self as contraction
chapters.description: Matthew explores selfing as a dense, contractive force and invites a relinquishing of ownership, proving, and defensiveness.

chapters.title: Clinging and hate
chapters.description: He describes clinging as complicated but simplifying when released, then turns to why hatred cannot ultimately work.

chapters.title: Delusion and mortality
chapters.description: The meditation widens to delusion, disappointment, and the turning toward death as a way of softening attention.

chapters.title: Putting things down
chapters.description: Drawing on Shinzen Young's phrase, Matthew closes by encouraging continued softening until awareness feels weightless.

title: Guided Meditation: Self and Willfulness
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that keeps returning to a simple question: what happens if we stop trying to make meditation happen? He gently examines how willfulness can create a self that manages experience, and invites a softer trust in awareness, breath, body, and sound. The practice gradually shifts from effort and suspicion toward surrender, letting doingness dissolve into openness.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing willfulness and trusting awareness instead of trying to control experience.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation begins with a brief welcome and settles into the instruction to meditate.

chapters.title: What Began Meditation
chapters.description: He asks listeners to look at what they do when they first begin meditating, then to notice the impulse to stop doing.

chapters.title: Trusting the Mind
chapters.description: The talk turns toward the suspicion that we must make something happen, and toward trusting mind and awareness instead.

chapters.title: The Self as Organizer
chapters.description: He reflects on how even simple attentional instructions can give rise to a self that orchestrates practice.

chapters.title: Anchor Everywhere
chapters.description: The teaching widens the sense of support, suggesting that what we trust may not depend on personal effort alone.

chapters.title: Willfulness and Job
chapters.description: He names willfulness as part of the self's work and questions the role we keep assigning ourselves in meditation.

chapters.title: Awareness Finds Us
chapters.description: The practice deepens into letting awareness meet us, rather than reaching for it through effort.

chapters.title: Surrender and Ease
chapters.description: The closing settles into surrender, asking practice to become a movement of trust and release.

title: Guided Meditation: Tranquility
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on tranquility as both settling and clearer seeing. He begins with breath, bodily ease, and releasing clinging, then widens the practice to listening to silence, resting in the visual field, and allowing attention to soften around experience. A brief reference to Mother Teresa’s description of prayer helps frame the practice as a quiet, receptive listening, without requiring any religious belief.
short_summary: A guided meditation that uses breath, bodily settling, silence, and visual openness to explore tranquility as a basis for clear, receptive awareness.

chapters.title: Tranquility and clarity
chapters.description: He introduces tranquility as both a calming quality and a way of clarifying the inner landscape.

chapters.title: Resting the body
chapters.description: The practice opens with acknowledging fatigue, lengthening the breath, and sensing into what is already resting in the body.

chapters.title: Softening clinging
chapters.description: He invites releasing tension and meeting grasping, aversion, and control with a gentler breath and a wider field.

chapters.title: Body as a resource
chapters.description: Repeated cues to soften and widen lead into finding a natural bodily zone that can serve as an anchor.

chapters.title: Listening for silence
chapters.description: Attention shifts from breath to silence, including distant sounds and the space around sound, as a live quality rather than the absence of noise.

chapters.title: Silent listening
chapters.description: Mother Teresa's line about prayer frames a contemplative listening that can be practiced without any religious belief.

chapters.title: Visual field and darkness
chapters.description: He turns to the visual field with closed eyes, noticing brightness, images, and the darkness behind them while resting attention gently.

chapters.title: Letting clinging wait
chapters.description: Near the close, he names the impulse to cling and suggests promising it a return later, not now.

chapters.title: Closing rest
chapters.description: The meditation ends in sustained quiet, with repeated gentle cues and little further instruction.

title: Guided Meditation: Goodnight Moon
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around the rhythm of Goodnight Moon, using the book’s repeated bedtime refrain as a gentle model for letting experience rest. He invites listeners to say goodnight to memories, pain, thoughts, and feelings, then to settle into breathing and open awareness without forcing anything away. The practice gradually widens from soft release into a quiet sense of presence, effortlessness, and brightness.
short_summary: A guided meditation that uses the rhythm of Goodnight Moon to practice gently putting down experience and resting in awareness.

chapters.title: Opening the Meditation
chapters.description: Matthew welcomes the group and introduces the evening’s meditation, then recalls Goodnight Moon as the image that will shape the practice.

chapters.title: Saying Goodnight
chapters.description: He turns the book’s repeated refrain into an instruction to bless and release memories, pain, thoughts, feelings, and other pieces of experience.

chapters.title: Settling With Breath
chapters.description: The practice shifts toward breathing as a steady place to land, with attention and awareness described as gentle and open.

chapters.title: Open Awareness
chapters.description: He explores how the breath can be felt either within attention or arising in spaciousness, and encourages bidding goodnight to whatever can be put down.

chapters.title: Not Rejection
chapters.description: Matthew clarifies that putting something down is not the same as exile or aversion, and points toward patient, non-grasping attention.

chapters.title: Resting in Presence
chapters.description: The meditation deepens into a quiet surrender of effort, with an emphasis on placing hope in this moment and allowing awareness to hold what remains.

chapters.title: Fatigue and Brightness
chapters.description: Near the end, he includes fatigue itself as something that can soothe the body, while awareness leaves a brightness in its wake.

title: Guided Meditation: Feeling
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation centered on feeling, beginning with breath and a gentle turn away from compulsive problem-solving. The practice moves through the affective roots of reactivity, the ways feeling gets turned into story, and the invitation to let experience unwind without feeding it with thought. Later he broadens into retreat-specific reflection on futurelessness, death, effort, boredom, and how to keep practice steady without turning effort into self-strain.
short_summary: A guided meditation on feeling, relinquishment, and steady effort, moving from breath awareness into retreat reflection and walking practice.

chapters.title: Settling With Breath
chapters.description: The meditation begins with exhalation, body awareness, and a gradual settling into the retreat sit.

chapters.title: Problems and Dissolving
chapters.description: Matthew contrasts problems that need solving with problems that dissolve when held in awareness.

chapters.title: Commitment To This Moment
chapters.description: He frames practice as a commitment to stay with what is here rather than keep escaping into fixing.

chapters.title: Wanting And Knowing
chapters.description: The talk explores how wanting shapes knowing, and how relinquishment changes what perception can reveal.

chapters.title: Feeling And Reactivity
chapters.description: He names common reactions like sleepiness, agitation, craving, ill will, and doubt as movements rooted in feeling.

chapters.title: Stories From Feeling
chapters.description: Matthew describes how we keep narrating experience, and invites feeling to be just feeling without making a story.

chapters.title: Non-Thinking And Release
chapters.description: He distinguishes thinking from coping and encourages non-conceptual awareness supported by the breath.

chapters.title: Clinging And Heaven
chapters.description: The instruction turns toward letting go of clinging and noticing how easily the mind can cross into suffering.

chapters.title: Futurelessness And Death
chapters.description: Matthew reflects on the thin line between heaven and hell, the heaviness of the future, and how death clarifies what matters.

title: Guided Meditation: Convergence of Insight and Love (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver frames the talk around the convergence of insight and love: mindfulness as a careful deconstruction of experience, and the brahma-viharas as the heart’s place to rest. He moves through the four “species of love” — loving-kindness, compassion, sympathetic joy, and equanimity — showing how each meets a different kind of life moment, from goodness to suffering to helplessness. The guided portion then turns inward, inviting listeners to stop measuring, sense what is needed, and offer phrases of goodwill that soften the heart rather than harden it.
short_summary: A guided meditation on how insight and the four brahma-viharas meet the realities of suffering, goodness, helplessness, and self-judgment.

chapters.title: Insight and love
chapters.description: Brensilver introduces the tension and overlap between mindfulness, wisdom, and loving-kindness, then shows why opening to experience requires trust and care.

chapters.title: The four abodes
chapters.description: He names the brahma-viharas as resting places of the heart and briefly maps each one to a different situation in practice and daily life.

chapters.title: Compassion and equanimity
chapters.description: The talk lingers on compassion as meeting suffering honestly, then on equanimity as remaining open when suffering cannot be fixed.

chapters.title: Letting love be home
chapters.description: He emphasizes that this path is not about pretending, but about discovering a love that feels natural and already near.

chapters.title: Dropping specialness
chapters.description: The guided meditation turns toward self-view, inviting listeners to stop comparing, stop measuring, and meet experience without making the self a special case.
